pending.body: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/cli_writer_lease.rs:327-339` collapses "multiple detached owners found" to `None`, and the resume/stop paths in `src/cli/run.rs:1658-1670`, `src/cli/run.rs:1835-1853`, and `src/cli/run.rs:1954-1957` treat `None` as "no stale owner". If a project has both an orphaned task-side lease reference and another stale CLI/worktree lease, `run resume` / `run stop` can reacquire the writer lock and proceed even though contradictory durable cleanup state still exists. Ambiguous detached-owner state needs to surface as a blocking error, not be silently ignored.

`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3642-3663` and `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:3698-3728` now require `run.pid` to be reclaimable before clearing `task.lease_id`. On the success path, though, `LeaseService::release()` has already released the writer lock and removed the lease record (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:63-77`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:332-422`), so another process can legitimately acquire the project and write a fresh `run.pid` in that gap. In that race, `remove_owned_run_pid_file()` returns `LeaseCleanupPartialFailure`, the finished task keeps its old `lease_id`, and later recovery falsely quarantines a task whose old resources were already fully cleaned up.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 10 -> Plan and Implement
